graphify_native_status.limitation: native extraction/build commands from the Graphify skill reference were not available
graphify_native_status.artifact_strategy: manual Graphify-style maps built from git, file reads, and test evidence

red_team_blockers.title: Upstream CLI lacks the current fallback-disable contract
red_team_blockers.evidence: No upstream/main match for disable-fallback-model or HERMES_DISABLE_FALLBACK_MODEL; current local chat help includes --disable-fallback-model; external adapter emits the flag and env var by default.
red_team_blockers.required_gate: Preserve the flag or replace it with an upstream-compatible hard-stop contract, then run external adapter testEnvironment against the cutover Hermes binary.

red_team_blockers.title: Direct merge has concrete conflicts in production-sensitive surfaces
red_team_blockers.evidence: git merge-tree --write-tree --name-only HEAD upstream/main exits 1 with conflicts in CLI, gateway, run_agent, hermes_state, config, ACP, pyproject, provider docs, and tests.
red_team_blockers.required_gate: Use an upstream-based cutover branch and require a conflict-retirement ledger per file.

red_team_blockers.title: Live Paperclip inventory is not yet a hard preflight
red_team_blockers.evidence: Existing docs require canary and guard, but not live counts for hermes_local agents, active/queued runs, heartbeats, scheduled wakes, provider approvals, and command paths.
red_team_blockers.required_gate: Snapshot live Paperclip DB/config and publish pre/post inventory before selecting the new Hermes command path.

red_team_blockers.title: Paid-provider denial is described but not mechanically proven
red_team_blockers.evidence: Paperclip requires MiniMax-first recovery and no post-MiniMax OpenAI/Claude/Gemini fallback without explicit approval; upstream fallback_providers can continue if configured.
red_team_blockers.required_gate: Add tests that simulate MiniMax exhaustion and assert a blocked result with no denied provider invocation absent approval.

red_team_blockers.title: State database migration and adapter accounting are unproven
red_team_blockers.evidence: Local hermes_state.py is schema 7; upstream is schema 16; the external adapter reads state.db usage/cost/session fields.
red_team_blockers.required_gate: Migrate a copied production state.db with upstream code and verify external adapter usage extraction plus source=paperclip session behavior.

surfaces.local_state: Fork patches cover OpenCode metadata, OpenCode Go alias handling, and a local stale-api-mode guard.
surfaces.upstream_state: Upstream has provider profiles, model-specific api_mode inference, and _provider_supports_explicit_api_mode.
surfaces.what_we_gain: Broader provider support, better base_url/api_mode isolation, OpenCode Zen/Go first-class profiles, and fewer fork-only provider patches.
surfaces.what_we_could_lose: A local MiniMax-specific regression could disappear if not carried into upstream tests.
surfaces.cutover_action: Use upstream runtime_provider.py and models.py, then port only missing regressions.
validation: Run upstream runtime provider tests.
Add or retain MiniMax stale-api-mode regression.
Add OpenCode Go Qwen alias/model-mode regression if upstream does not already cover the exact alias.

surfaces.local_state: Paperclip policy treats MiniMax as the first automatic recovery lane and stops after MiniMax exhaustion.
surfaces.upstream_state: MiniMax, MiniMax CN, and MiniMax OAuth are first-class provider profiles with Anthropic-compatible defaults and prompt-cache handling.
surfaces.what_we_gain: Cleaner MiniMax routing and less custom provider code.
surfaces.what_we_could_lose: Upstream's generic fallback chain can continue beyond MiniMax if configured that way.
surfaces.cutover_action: Adopt upstream MiniMax provider code, but enforce a Paperclip adapter gate that blocks after MiniMax failure unless explicit paid-tier approval is present.
validation: Simulate primary provider failure.
Simulate MiniMax unavailable/exhausted.
Assert blocked result artifact is written.
Assert no OpenAI, Claude, Gemini, or other paid provider client is invoked without approval.

surfaces.local_state: Local fork added OpenCode provider metadata and Qwen OA-compatible alias handling.
surfaces.upstream_state: OpenCode Zen and OpenCode Go are bundled model-provider profiles with model-specific routing for Claude, Codex, GPT, MiniMax, GLM, Kimi, DeepSeek, and Qwen-family cache markers.
surfaces.what_we_gain: Official provider profile behavior, token caps for known models, and model-derived api_mode rather than stale persisted api_mode.
surfaces.what_we_could_lose: A local Paperclip-specific model alias might not be covered by upstream naming normalization.
surfaces.cutover_action: Prefer upstream OpenCode Go profile and add an alias compatibility test for any exact Paperclip model slug.
validation: Run OpenCode Go provider resolution tests.
Assert Qwen-family models use the intended OpenAI-compatible mode and cache marker path.
Assert Anthropic-wire models strip trailing /v1 when needed.

surfaces.local_state: Local fork includes fallback disable control.
surfaces.upstream_state: Upstream has fallback_providers and a fallback CLI manager, but no observed equivalent to a Paperclip hard stop after MiniMax.
surfaces.what_we_gain: Ordered fallback chains across CLI, gateway, cron, and subagents.
surfaces.what_we_could_lose: Paperclip could spend or run against disallowed providers if upstream fallback is accepted unguarded.
surfaces.cutover_action: Implement a Paperclip-specific fallback policy layer rather than relying on upstream global fallback semantics.
validation: Test no fallback configured.
Test MiniMax-only fallback.
Test OpenAI/Claude/Gemini fallback denied without approval.
Test explicit approval permits only the named approved provider and records the approval source.

surfaces.local_state: portfolio_os_adapter provides validate-bundle, dry-run, dispatch, status, and resume.
surfaces.upstream_state: No Portfolio-OS or Paperclip adapter was found on upstream/main.
surfaces.what_we_gain: Can re-home adapter as a plugin using upstream CLI command registration.
surfaces.what_we_could_lose: Direct upstream adoption deletes adapter package, docs, and wrapper.
surfaces.cutover_action: Create bundled or explicitly enabled plugin that registers hermes portfolio-os through ctx.register_cli_command.
validation: hermes portfolio-os --help exposes all current subcommands.
Existing adapter tests pass on upstream branch.
Paperclip can invoke the new command path without changing bundle payloads.

surfaces.local_state: The cockpit registers /Users/mnm/Documents/Github/hermes-paperclip-adapter as type hermes_local version 0.1.0.
surfaces.upstream_state: Hermes upstream does not include or replace this external Paperclip adapter.
surfaces.what_we_gain: Can point the adapter at a fresher Hermes core once the CLI contract is preserved.
surfaces.what_we_could_lose: Paperclip runs can fail if the new Hermes command path, --disable-fallback-model flag, provider flags, session behavior, or state.db usage accounting changes.
surfaces.cutover_action: Keep the external adapter installed, update its command path only after the upstream Hermes environment passes adapter tests, and preserve the hermes_local type contract.
validation: Run npm test in /Users/mnm/Documents/Github/hermes-paperclip-adapter.
Confirm adapter-plugins.json still registers type hermes_local.
Run createServerAdapter().testEnvironment against the new Hermes command.
Confirm the adapter still emits --disable-fallback-model by default or uses an equivalent upstream-safe guard.

surfaces.local_state: Validates pos.hermes_task_bundle.v1, mandate types, tasks, safety, allowed roots, and target existence.
surfaces.upstream_state: No equivalent contract layer was found.
surfaces.what_we_gain: No upstream replacement; all value comes from preserving the current contract.
surfaces.what_we_could_lose: Invalid or unsafe Paperclip bundles could execute if validation is weakened.
surfaces.cutover_action: Port contract.py unchanged first, then improve only with backward-compatible checks.
validation: Golden valid bundle passes.
Wrong schema version fails.
Forbidden operation fails.
Outside allowed roots fails.
local_only plus push fails.

surfaces.local_state: Launch execution requires Internet Pipes readiness alpha_ready or factory_ready and no missing stations.
surfaces.upstream_state: No equivalent upstream readiness gate was found.
surfaces.what_we_gain: Paperclip execution remains tied to the current business readiness model.
surfaces.what_we_could_lose: Launch bundles with incomplete generation, validation, evaluation, differentiation, visualization, or recommendation stations could dispatch.
surfaces.cutover_action: Keep Internet Pipes validation in the Paperclip plugin contract layer.
validation: alpha_ready launch with no missing stations passes.
launch_execution with missing station fails.
evidence.internet_pipes mismatch fails.

surfaces.local_state: Adapter writes dry-run status, result JSON, execution log, QA status, blockers, next_actions, Paperclip ids, and GStack artifacts.
surfaces.upstream_state: No equivalent Paperclip result schema exists.
surfaces.what_we_gain: Can formalize schema as plugin output contract.
surfaces.what_we_could_lose: Paperclip cockpit may lose status visibility or misread a run as healthy when required fields disappear.
surfaces.cutover_action: Add fixture-based artifact schema tests and keep field names stable.
validation: Dry-run artifact equals current schema.
Completed dispatch artifact equals current schema.
Blocked dispatch artifact includes auditable blocker and no false success.

surfaces.local_state: The external adapter lists live OpenCode Go and Zen models, hides unsafe choices, requires deepseek-v4-pro/deepseek-v4-flash on Go and deepseek-v4-flash-free on Zen, and maps qwen3.7-max to deepseek-v4-pro.
surfaces.upstream_state: Upstream provider profiles improve OpenCode routing but do not replace Paperclip's UI/runtime model availability gate.
surfaces.what_we_gain: Upstream OpenCode Go support can reduce local Hermes routing patches.
surfaces.what_we_could_lose: Paperclip may advertise unavailable or unsafe models if external adapter checks are dropped because upstream provider support appears sufficient.
surfaces.cutover_action: Keep external adapter model discovery and required-lane checks until a live canary proves upstream provider discovery covers the same Paperclip contract.
validation: Mock Go/Zen /models endpoints and run adapter tests.
Verify qwen3.7-max remains excluded from selectable Hermes oa-compat candidates.
Verify required missing models make testEnvironment fail before a real Hermes run starts.

surfaces.local_state: Paperclip policy requires pausing the Hermes adapter path when inference availability or provider recovery fails.
surfaces.upstream_state: Generic fallback recovery is not a Paperclip scheduler pause mechanism.
surfaces.what_we_gain: A clearer separation between model fallback and operational dispatch safety.
surfaces.what_we_could_lose: Scheduled Paperclip runs could keep firing into a broken or disallowed provider state.
surfaces.cutover_action: Add a Paperclip plugin failure state that writes a blocked result and emits or records an adapter pause signal consumed by Paperclip.
validation: Inference preflight failure writes blocked result.
MiniMax exhaustion writes blocked result.
Paperclip scheduler or cockpit records the pause condition.
Manual resume requires explicit operator action.

surfaces.local_state: The external adapter has testEnvironment model checks and selected-provider completion probes; the Python Portfolio-OS adapter does not yet have an equivalent pre-dispatch probe.
surfaces.upstream_state: Upstream validates provider config in many places but does not know Paperclip dispatch semantics.
surfaces.what_we_gain: Prevents bundle execution from starting when provider routing is already known bad.
surfaces.what_we_could_lose: A bundle could mutate a target repo before discovering the configured inference path is unavailable.
surfaces.cutover_action: Keep external adapter preflight behavior and add pre-dispatch provider resolution plus optional lightweight live probe to the Paperclip/Portfolio plugin before target repo mutation.
validation: Missing required provider credential blocks before target repo mutation.
Stale api_mode is corrected or rejected before dispatch.
Live probe can be disabled in tests and enabled in production canary.

surfaces.local_state: Local fork patched ACP auth method schema compatibility.
surfaces.upstream_state: Upstream has broader ACP auth/session support and tests.
surfaces.what_we_gain: Official ACP compatibility and richer startup/auth capabilities.
surfaces.what_we_could_lose: Any local client relying on the old exact auth payload should be smoke-tested.
surfaces.cutover_action: Use upstream ACP code and run current ACP tests plus a local client smoke.
validation: Run upstream ACP auth tests.
Launch ACP adapter against the local editor/client path.
Confirm configured-provider and terminal-setup auth methods show correctly.

surfaces.local_state: Local fork has output.max_sentences, output.max_chars, HERMES_OUTPUT_MAX_* vars, compacted response suffix, and tests across CLI, gateway, ACP, delegate, streaming, cron, RL.
surfaces.upstream_state: Upstream has max_output_tokens and provider cap handling but not the exact compact-response contract.
surfaces.what_we_gain: Upstream token handling is more mature at provider-request level.
surfaces.what_we_could_lose: Compact Paperclip artifacts or operator responses may become longer and less predictable.
surfaces.cutover_action: Prefer adapter-local shaping for Paperclip artifacts unless global compact output is still a product requirement.
validation: If adapter-local: assert result/log artifacts are bounded.
If global: port current tests to upstream conversation-loop architecture.
Confirm prompt caching is not broken by post-hoc context mutation.

surfaces.local_state: Local venv reports Hermes 0.4.0 and has no python -m pip module.
surfaces.upstream_state: Upstream uses exact pins, uv.lock, plugin package data, and Python >=3.11,<3.14.
surfaces.what_we_gain: Reproducible dependency set and smaller supply-chain blast radius.
surfaces.what_we_could_lose: Mutating the old venv in place is brittle and may fail without pip.
surfaces.cutover_action: Create a fresh upstream environment using upstream's documented uv/install path.
validation: hermes --version reports 0.16.0 cutover build.
python import smoke for core packages passes.
Plugin package data and Paperclip plugin are included in editable and wheel installs.

surfaces.local_state: Paperclip command is available through local wrapper.
surfaces.upstream_state: Standalone plugins are opt-in through plugins.enabled; built-in model-provider plugins are handled separately.
surfaces.what_we_gain: Paperclip integration can be isolated from core CLI code.
surfaces.what_we_could_lose: Command disappears if plugin is not enabled or bundled correctly.
surfaces.cutover_action: Add setup/migration step that enables the Paperclip plugin for the production Hermes profile.
validation: hermes plugins list shows Paperclip plugin enabled.
hermes portfolio-os --help works in the same environment Paperclip uses.
HERMES_SAFE_MODE behavior is documented as disabling plugin command availability.

surfaces.local_state: Current Paperclip use depends on adapter CLI and cockpit execution, not upstream's full gateway/plugin platform expansion.
surfaces.upstream_state: Gateway/platform surfaces expanded substantially and moved toward plugins.
surfaces.what_we_gain: More supported adapters and operational surfaces.
surfaces.what_we_could_lose: New services, ports, or platform hooks could collide with Paperclip cockpit assumptions if enabled blindly.
surfaces.cutover_action: Leave unrelated gateway/platform plugins disabled during first cutover unless Paperclip explicitly needs them.
validation: List enabled plugins.
Check active ports/processes before and after canary.
Run Paperclip guard after enabling only required surfaces.

surfaces.local_state: Docs under docs/portfolio_os define adapter contract and upgrade decisions, but upstream deletes them unless reintroduced.
surfaces.upstream_state: Upstream has plugin developer docs but no Paperclip-specific operator runbook.
surfaces.what_we_gain: Can align Paperclip docs with official plugin terminology.
surfaces.what_we_could_lose: Operators lose the exact contract and rollback instructions.
surfaces.cutover_action: Port docs into the Paperclip plugin docs or docs/portfolio_os in the cutover branch.
validation: Docs cover installation, enablement, commands, bundle schema, fallback policy, canary, rollback, and hard stops.
Commands in docs are exercised in tests or canary.

surfaces.local_state: Focused local tests pass for runtime provider and Portfolio-OS adapter.
surfaces.upstream_state: Upstream test suite is much larger and organized under newer paths.
surfaces.what_we_gain: More coverage for provider routing, fallback, ACP, packaging, gateway, and plugins.
surfaces.what_we_could_lose: A green old focused suite is not enough to prove the upstream cutover.
surfaces.cutover_action: Run layered tests on the cutover branch before live canary.
validation: Runtime provider tests.
Provider fallback tests.
Paperclip plugin CLI tests.
Bundle contract tests.
Adapter artifact golden tests.
ACP auth tests.
Full non-integration suite before production.

surfaces.local_state: Paperclip currently relies on live state, receipts, and guard evidence.
surfaces.upstream_state: Upstream cannot prove Paperclip correctness without a Paperclip canary.
surfaces.what_we_gain: Real confidence that adapter, provider policy, and cockpit visibility survived the upgrade.
surfaces.what_we_could_lose: Repo mutations, missing status artifacts, or unauthorized provider attempts if the canary is skipped.
surfaces.cutover_action: Run staged canary: validate fixture, dry-run, disposable target dispatch, real low-risk target no-push dispatch, then guard.
validation: All canary artifacts have expected schema.
Provider logs show allowed provider path only.
Paperclip cockpit guard is green after the new Hermes path is selected.

surfaces.local_state: Current main and venv remain available.
surfaces.upstream_state: Cutover branch should be separate from current production path.
surfaces.what_we_gain: Fast recovery if upstream cutover fails a hard stop.
surfaces.what_we_could_lose: In-place replacement could strand Paperclip without a known-good Hermes path.
surfaces.cutover_action: Do not mutate current main/venv for production until upstream branch passes canary; keep Paperclip path switch reversible.
validation: Rollback command/path documented.
Old ./bin/hermes path still works before final switch.
Hard-stop failures trigger rollback rather than partial operation.

recommended_cutover_summary: Base the new branch on upstream/main.
Rebuild the environment from upstream dependency rules.
Port Paperclip/Portfolio-OS adapter as an upstream plugin.
Adopt upstream provider runtime, MiniMax, OpenCode Go, ACP, and packaging.
Preserve Paperclip-specific bundle, artifact, Internet Pipes, inference preflight, adapter pause, and fallback hard-stop policy.
Run layered tests and a live Paperclip canary before switching production.
